name: ETH State Change Momentum Hold v1
thesis: In ETH 1h transition conditions, edge can emerge when a weak state improves and price holds above regained structure instead of instantly fading. The signal should come from broad state-change confirmation and post-break hold, not exact breakout hero timing.
edge_mechanism: state improvement followed by momentum hold
invalidation_condition: Abort if walk-forward testing shows zero trades on valid data, contradictory filter behavior, weak blind-window participation, or no post-cost edge.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Transition from weak structure into directional continuation after the new state is held rather than instantly faded.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: State shifts on ETH 1h can produce short persistence bursts when structure is regained and not immediately rejected.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 1h is active enough for repeated state changes while still giving enough bars for denser participation than slower lanes.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the lane is actually trend-down with little reclaim behavior, post-break hold entries may under-participate or reverse quickly.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If participation exists but density is weak, relax the hold distance before changing management logic.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; hold, state, and structure filters all point in the same direction
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: reduced by using broad hold thresholds rather than exact breakout bars

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.3 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.3 * ATR_14
